notes.total: 157 tickets scoped across 7 sprints — healthy pipeline indicating consistent team capacity utilisation throughout the quarter.
notes.completed: 145 of 157 tickets completed, with only 3 still in progress at quarter end — strong delivery against scope.
notes.completion_rate: 92% completion rate is excellent and demonstrates effective sprint planning and execution discipline.
notes.releases_shipped: 11 releases shipped across the quarter shows strong deployment cadence, though last release date of 2026-02-03 appears to be a data error.
notes.oos_total: 29 out-of-sprint tickets (18% of total) indicates some reactive work, but remains within acceptable bounds for planning discipline.
notes.oos_open: All out-of-sprint tickets resolved — no unplanned debt carried beyond quarter end.
notes.type_split: Bug ratio at 19% (30 of 157) is healthy, with the majority of work focused on feature delivery through stories and tasks.
notes.avg_releases: Median of 1 release per sprint indicates most sprints shipped at least once, with the mean of 1.6 showing some sprints delivered multiple releases — solid deployment rhythm.
notes.time_logged: Only 435.8 hours logged against 1,950 estimated hours is a severe time tracking issue — 81% of completed work has no time data, making capacity planning unreliable.
notes.estimate_accuracy: 22% accuracy with -78% variance indicates chronic underestimation, though this is distorted by the poor time logging discipline noted above.
notes.no_estimate: 33 tickets (21%) completed without estimates reduces planning reliability — enforce estimation hygiene in sprint planning.
notes.rollover: 26 tickets (17%) rolled over from previous sprints indicates some predictability issues, though not severe — review causes to reduce sprint churn.
notes.cycle_time: Median cycle time of 21 days is concerning for flow, with the mean of 45 days revealing significant outliers dragging tickets through the pipeline — identify and address blockers causing extended cycle times.
notes.assignee_workload: Workload is heavily concentrated with Ivor Didovic (31 tickets) and Riccardo Fachin (41 tickets, non-team), while Sandor Kocsis completed only 1 of 4 assigned tickets — investigate capacity constraints and redistribute work more evenly.
